title: Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez
text:
Note:

Hi, I am Abusyeed, and I did this study for my personal purpose. My role in this study is that of a UX designer with a duration of 2–3 days, and this study focuses on the redesign of the Coinpedia website in terms of both the user interface and the user experience.

Thanks for considering my application, Shruthi.Happy reading!

## 01 Introduction

I guess you are familiar with these cryptography and web3-related technologies; they have a lot of data, and I really don’t know which numerical data is important, to be honest. After doing intensive research, I started making UI screens in my mind, then I started playing with the rectangles and made all components one by one in random order, then stacked them as per IA. The IA that I read helped me to arrange everything in a visual hierarchy, and I did a good job in terms of interaction too. So let's dive deeper to learn more about my work.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Frame_44.png)

### Challenge

Coinpedia is a trusted cryptocurrency, event, and information service agency for blockchain technology and overall digital assets. Moreover, this is an entirely independent platform that covers blockchain, cryptocurrency, and decentralized applications. And much other crypto information from the next-generation web. The challenge is to redesign the main page of Coinpedia and a currency page called Bitcoin. It has mentioned to redesign completely by colors, typography, imagery, and the way I can do it creatively, but it should be done in a way that increases user visit rate, accessibility, and usability too.

### Goal

The goal is to make users retrain back on the website, create interactions, and make them comfortable. I will be achieving this by observing the whole website, writing down the errors, designing components, and placing them in a structured way.

## 02 Initial Findings

Initially, when I saw this website, I liked the aesthetics part, as they are using dark blue. It actually gives a lot of trust and stability. For instance, if someone is going to invest in a crypto currency and searches for some better stat sites that can give a lot of information about the currency, they may land on Coinpedia. There is a chance that he will get comforted about crypto and all. They also have small help icons to help out with what and why types of questions to clarify users doubts.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Untitled.png)

Market page of Coinpedia - Tab version

Secondly, I saw those fonts, and I didn’t like them much, but the designer who did this must have done it with the intention of improving readability in non-display sizes. I knew this font before; it is called Space Grotesk. This made a few numbers confusing due to the typeface.

I have seen a lot of errors on this website and lags due to the high dynamic data, which is changing every 5 minutes. There is a lag in the interaction part too, which I have fixed by changing its complex layout to a simpler one. I didn’t feel much needed to be changed. So I only fixed and gave a newly polished website with enhancements in fonts, visual appeal, and accessibility.

### Target Audience

Investors

Millennials

Gen Z

Gamers

## 03 Ideation - Re-design

Whenever I do redesign any digital product, I always follow this rule at first pace;

“Usually Choose Incremental Changes Over a Major Overhaul” - NN group

As I said before in the overview, there are a few minimum changes that can enhance the webpages very much and actually create impact. I started with the navbar itself; initially, the menus were placed very left, and there I found two types of buttons: portfolio tracking and login. I changed all these in the most minimal way possible.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Untitled 1.png)

Old Design - Market

Generally, whenever redesigns of webpages are done, they always do user research first to structure the audience and users who are using them, and they will design a user-centric approach to drive a better result by specifically targeting the audience, but in this case, I am doing an assessment and I have time constraints, So I didn’t go there.

### Wireframe

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/wireframe.png)

Wireframe

## 04 Screens

### Market Place Page

Figma Link - https://www.figma.com/file/6ZkoOapePsdkOyufWe2AFJ/Coinpedia?type=design&node-id=86%3A124&mode=design&t=jeAIhcvEIijAcTUs-1

In this link you will find everything related to new design of both Coinpedia and Bitcoin page.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/mp.png)

New Design - Market

Search Bar

I have changed almost all types of buttons and made the contrast color look aesthetically appealing. The very first change I made was the search bar. There are a lot of cryptocurrencies on the market, but not all the top-ranked ones are the ones in which users invest. Users have very own thoughts about investing in cryptocurrencies, which they can find in the list, but there should be a bigger search to give space to users so they can investigate, learn, and find new insights from cryptocurrencies.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/ovl.png)

Search Bar

- The CTA buttons I was talking about are visible in the above image. The primary function in the navbar can be the login button and the currency they want to view, because it will attract the users to login once they learn that multiple currencies can be changed. That is very obvious, as the information sites do, but it will give it a boost.

“Track your portfolio” has been shifted down to use it efficiently by the 6th law of usability heuristics to recognition rather than recall, which is to make options visible and lower the cognitive load on users. I learned that, just like iterating design, I always learn from the feedback given. Thanks, Shruti, for your feedback.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/nav1.png)

Navbar Changes - Coinpedia

- Dark mode and the Notifications button don’t work on the real webpage, and they are also very irrelevant in a navbar without logging in. It can be placed in the account details when a user gets into an account.

- This is the newly designed button and dropdown, and it clearly shows one is to change currency or country and a login CTA button.

- The crypto currency details are placed as a design of the stock market, with rolling text exposing multiple currency details in a row.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/nav2.png)

Cryptocurrencies - Coinpedia

- When I tested this website, it still wishes me even at noon. Actually, it doesn’t create any impact between the users, and it also needs to be dynamic, so the market cap is fixed and wishing users have been removed.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/nav3.png)

Remarks - Coinpedia

- I have used very legible icons in this website redesign to make it easier to understand. The CTA in the below container has been changed to the CTA color to make users feel like it is a clickable button. The colors are not consistent enough, which are green and red.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/container.png)

- Coming to the category part, I have changed to filter because it actually functions like a search. To make a simple interaction, I converted it into a drop-down menu so users can easily tap and select to learn more.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/er1.png)

### Bitcoin Page

Figma Link - https://www.figma.com/file/6ZkoOapePsdkOyufWe2AFJ/Coinpedia?type=design&node-id=86%3A124&mode=design&t=jeAIhcvEIijAcTUs-1 (Same)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/BTC.png)

BTC Page

As per the requirement, I also have to redesign a currency page. When I saw it for the first time, it was not at the point due to gaps, and a few features weren’t working properly. I then started designing in random order for specific components like supply, indicator sentiment, and community support.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Untitled 2.png)

Old Design - Coinpedia

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Untitled 3.png)

Old Design - Coinpedia

Containers

The page already had a lot of graphs and charts stating the numerical data in a visualization mode because it would be easy for users to grasp the picture. As a data science student, I have done data visualization using the Tableau tool, and there are specific reasons to use each chart.

Supply

As the chart is a little bit confusing but the stats are clear, it is good for going through the bar charts only because three variables are there, so it will depict a clear picture of supply stats.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/chart1.png)

Supply Redesign

Indicator

The sentiment indicator is present on all analysis pages. I designed it in a minimal way as a speed-o-meter with a box that also indicates the result of a strong buy down the thorn. In the previous design, the speed o-meter was used from Figma’s community. I knew this and have seen it before, but I changed to a moderate-colored meter.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/chart2.png)

Indicator Sentiment Redesign

Community Trust

The community trust has low visibility in old design. It is very important for informative and fintech websites to showcase others perspectives to invest in a currency for growth. It will always influence, and this will surely help early adopters to invest without any hesitation. That’s why I came up with a new design to encourage the voting system for currencies.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/chart3.png)

Community Trust Redesign

I guess you are also aware of a Japanese tradition from called bento boxes, which they usually use in a lunch box to pack their lunch in a versatile and even variety of food. The same principle was applied when I replaced it with the above images, which feature currency infographics. The purpose of using bento box design is to make responsive websites, and it actually works like a charm.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Untitled 4.png)

Sticky BTC details

I have changed all the clickable primary buttons from a grayish texture to the CTA’s button color with new filled icons; these UI changes are listed in the style guide. I have designed only the main parts of pages like charts and prices, but the website down the line is stable, so I didn’t go far.

## 05 Styles

I used a sans-serif typeface for better readability, and it is always suggested for fintech information websites to clearly showcase the numerical data. I also used layout grids to maintain visual order, and they are focused on the center of the website. The font is very legible and mostly used in many websites for avoiding issues in low-resolution part, these type of fonts gives modern and clean communication.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Styles.png)

Styles

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Coinpedia - Re-design - Ultimez/Untitled 5.png)

Layout grid.

So, by using the layout grid and Figma's rulers, the information is organized and there is an equal spacing between the containers, which aids in the conversion to mobile format.

## 06 Conclusion

Lastly, I would conclude that the enhancements to the website features of Coinpedia have been discussed, and the usability aspects have been covered. My findings highlight the UX mentioned in the redesign study. These opportunities will not only maintain existing users but will also attract a larger audience.

Thanks for reading till the end!

Figma

https://www.figma.com/file/6ZkoOapePsdkOyufWe2AFJ/Coinpedia?type=design&node-id=0%3A1&mode=design&t=jeAIhcvEIijAcTUs-1

title: Competitive Audit - Real Estate sites
text:
### Introduction

In this competitive analysis, I am going to share insights on real estate websites like 99Acre, Housing, and Magicbricks and their SWOT analysis. As they feature, almost everyone can access them as they are working for more than 100 websites, and yes, I am going to list out everything that I found as a insight. I will try to make all the positives and negatives unbiased.

I have heard that housing.com in recent days, like a few years ago, and 99acres are the real O.G. of digital real estate, but actually they lack the latest feature trends. We will see all these in a detailed manner. Although people use 99acres rather than housing because it has legit listings, housing has tons of fraudulent listings. I read this in the Quora forum.

Every popular real estate website has a backed-up company or has partners from the past; the most known are 99acre from Naukri Group and Magicbricks from Times Group.

Note : I am doing this for my personal purpose as a research task. The duration of this task is 2 days, and my role here in this study is only to analyze the best and most missed features that they provide to users in terms of user experience.

All this testing and exploring of websites is done through incognito profiles not from cached personal profiles to extract the actual content.

### Goal

To make this more informative and to extract insights from the websites, we will go through the SWOT analysis and competitive research on those websites. In terms of the features, I will go through the user experience at first. Following that, I will do a competitive UX audit with categories specifically.

### Website Evaluation

These three websites are the target, and I have collected a generic feature list in which the real estate companies should basically focus on these things.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Competitive Audit - Real Estate sites/logo.png)

Market Analysis

Annual Revenue> $1B$25M - $50M> $1B

Partners or CompaniesNaukriTimesRea, Makaan, Proptiger

Screenshots

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Competitive Audit - Real Estate sites/Untitled.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Competitive Audit - Real Estate sites/Untitled 1.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Competitive Audit - Real Estate sites/Untitled 2.png)

Impressions - UI

After going through the first impressions on all three websites, it feels like housing.com has done significant work in designing their website. I really like their aesthetics and visual appeal. Coming to 99acres, I felt a good design with what a user actually needed from their end. They do have headers with a slight animated dropdown, and it is seamless. In Magicbricks, the fonts are in regular styles, which feel a little odd compared to them. The red-colored layout matches the brand name and identity. It is not up to par when compared with other sites.

### Competitive Analysis

I have made this more simple form to provide an overview of all these websites in three sections, respectively. This process can also be called competitive user experience auditing, where we can understand the sole purpose and features of the concerned companies.

- Information & Stats

- Features & Interface

- Flow & Visuals

All these testing where done by specific prompts such as

“Simran is new trainee in her new job in Bengaluru, she is in search of PG under 20,000 with all amenities”

“Tawfiq always gets a contract job for 2-3 months in a city, Now he is Delhi and he is search of studio apartments on rent also near to his client”

“Mugilan got new promotion to Hyderabad office, he needed a 2 BHK house on rent under 40,000 which is newly possessed and furnished near KG schools”

This will surely make to me to search to reach the goal in the same time to analyze the process how they work.

Fun fact

I increased 5% website traffic in these companies in this competitive analysis and by research prompts. Now I am currently getting tons of property ads on my social media. I don’t know how they track me, even if I was using incognito; I guess the ads relay through my IP.

### SWOT Analysis

I have made a competitive analysis and found verdicts which I will discuss in the SWOT analysis in more detailed way.

### Strengths

📌

Housing and 99acre have good visual aesthetics and Color usage.

📌

Magicbricks have reasonable pricing for connecting sellers of INR 1099/30 contacts one time.

📌

Housing has language support and better accessibility by using semantic colors.

📌

Magicbricks have Chatbot support for instant help.

📌

Housing have Edge features like loan, credit related which makes more users to get attention.

### Weaknesses

📌

All these websites lags in mobile view, but 99acres is better than others in this fact.

📌

99acres lacks in navigating tasks for new users.

📌

Magicbricks user interface got to be enhanced more on fonts and layouts.

📌

Housing’s images for properties are not consistent either 2D or 3D.

📌

International properties are broken links in Magicbricks and 99acre.

📌

Poor app marketing

### Opportunities

📌

Providing better navbar with exactly what users need.

📌

Adding and improving properties from 3rd Tier cities.

📌

Chatbot integration and AI enabled sites to drive more insights.

📌

Using D-apps or Gen AI can remove scam listings

📌

Onboarding new users or a hint tutorial can be introduced.

📌

Accessibility in terms of fonts, color contrast and magnifying capabilities.

### Threats

📌

Housing has lot of features but very less people who built trust will use.

📌

Lots of fraudulent and scams through these platforms are made.

📌

Sites majorly targets age groups of 25 - 34, which lacks trust of above 34.

📌

There are more other specific need competitors than these sites.

📌

Average visit duration is getting low due to inefficient user flow.

📌

There are more other specific need competitors like commonfloor than these sites.

### Conclusion

My findings highlight the importance of ongoing development in terms of UX, mobile optimization, and features in mentioned in the audit. These opportunities will not only maintain existing users, but will also attract a larger audience, with long-term growth and competitiveness in the real estate market.Thanks for reading till the end and have a great day!

title: FoundIt - UX Case Study
text:
Note:

Hi I am Abusyeed currently doing bachelors in AI and DS. I am making this research for an event, then I thought to do for my portfolio as well. The event is a UI/UX Design contest from Zuno website and I found quite a few things which is actually not necessary or it can be ignored. Whenever brand changes the people still stick with the past experiences with website. Therefore the changes don’t reach their expectations , So I tried to suggest few of my insights.

## 01 Introduction

We all know how monster.com, a legacy job search portal vanished by Indeed.com. The monster website went on low stocks due to the unfulfillment of the founders' vision of making Profiles instead of resumes and not making good user experience trends. This downfall is because they thought of applying the algorithm of LinkedIn to create profiles and make the users stick with the website even after not using the Monster website back in the day. They also lost their way by not efficiently using data and the internet, So the competitors filled the void with data and networking. After ages, Quess took the monster and rebranded as Foundit, a Website for freshers and job seekers to search for jobs and internships with a layout like LinkedIn, Glassdoor, and Indeed. Here, I will go through this website and review my insights into this case study.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/FoundIt - UX Case Study/Group_32.png)

Sketching points and flow

### Problem Statement

The challenge is to design a successful landing page for Foundit that effectively communicates the organization's message and provides an optimal user experience. In order to accomplish this, a thorough understanding of the organization's mission, target audience, and desired user experience is necessary. The website lacks responsiveness and does not effectively highlight job search functionality on the landing page. Additionally, the website lags behind its competitors in user experience and marketing efforts

### Goal

The goal is to refine the website to meet the needs of its target audience, including professionals, freshers, and recruiters. For a better analysis of this website as it has changed from Monster to foundIt, I will be recording this way better by observations , statistics , persona and all the aspects which causes this new website to standout among the competitors.

### Core Objective of Case Study

- Record Observations

- UX Recommendations

- Design Concepts

- Key Persona

- Suggestions

## 02 Website Evaluation

My Analysis - First Impressions

First of all, the website is not responsive. I came to know this when I did testing throughout the website. Secondly , I went through the landing page of FoundIt where I can barely see the focus to hit a job search. From the first impressions of the website, it wasn't good as it looks like a faulty website. I took this as a problem statement as I like to change things that existed than to start from scratch.

### Challenges

- About the experience , little lagging behind because its competitors are far quicker than Found it

- Landing page is not making into focus to search jobs , log in and registering users are placed with a ham icon in desktop site itself

-  Monster Inc is missing that can take this brand to bigger level

- No proper marketing on the mobile app

- Popular searches or Recent jobs missing

### Target Audience

- Professionals

- Freshers

- Companies (Talent & Recruiters)

### Needs

- Freshers can seek paid internships as those are main ways of getting motivation to work through Zuno , Which is a side start of Foundit.

- To get a better experience to the user

- All devices

- Services can be in landing page for better viewing and accessing

## 03 Market Analysis

Competitive Analysis

- Glassdoor

- Indeed

- LinkedIn

- Wellfound

Assumptions & Consideration

I assume foundIt is making their website far better by this event , So the considering the devices they use and all. The branding is an addon point to them because people still stick with older websites as they do their services good enough. It is easier to find a job by using its own services like resume builder and LinkedIn Makeover , So there are some scope of refining the website. Also they are looking into the the ecosystem too.

## 04 The Approach

Design Process

- Empathize

- Define

- Ideate

- Prototype

- Test

## Empathize

In these Five stages of approach this can achieve a better landing page by making the job search to the focus. Although the empathize phase should be as end user exhaustive testing but , we can go with the online sources which forms qualitative as well as quantitative user research. This is basically full of assumptions and considerations.

## Define

First of all we need to understand our users need by defining the type of users , this would be most important factor while developing a product. I have defined 4 types of user persona.

4 User Persona

- Absolute 3rd Year

- 5 Years XP

- Leech

- Service Alone

### Absolute 3rd Year

- I need a paid internship

- I need experience and exposure from a company

- I need at least 3 LPA

Mindset

Name - Harish E

Age - 20

Harish is a 3rd year undergraduate student who is trying to secure a job in a tech-based MNC for a design fresher job after completing his degree in four years. However, he is afraid of not performing well in certain rounds of the interview process. As a result, he has found it helpful to participate in mock interviews to improve his chances of getting a good job as a fresher.

### 5 Years XP

- I am bored of working here , I don’t like this work culture

- I need 8% hike for my role

- I need to change my field

Mindset

Name - Vishnu

Age - 25

Vishnu is a UI/UX designer in a company and also has worked as intern as well as full time employee with a good design knowledge but he is facing financial issues as he is not getting well paid in current role so he need a change of field or good company with good salary hike. He lives in a metropolitical city so he need to manage his expenses as well

### Leech

- I have worked 2 decades in this company , surely I will get 2X

- I will join some high designation role in core company

Mindset

Name - Madhusudanan

Age - 48

Madhusudanan has been a long-time employee of a company, having worked there for two decades. He is hoping to obtain a significant salary increase or a high-level position within the organization. He is working same as position he came before 20 years , He needs a change.

### Services Alone

- I need a resume which can pass with a application tracking system

- I need certified courses

Mindset

Name - Mugilan

Age - 20

Mugilan is a young graduate with a degree in BSc, who has just started his job search. As a fresher, he is looking for a website that can provide him with an easy-to-use platform that can help him create an ATS-free resume. Mugilan is aware of the importance of creating a good first impression with his resume, and he wants to ensure that his skills and experience are accurately reflected.

### Empathy Map

An empathy map can help to gain insights into what users see and do, hear, think, and feel when interacting with a product or service. This can be achieved by conducting user research, such as interviews or observations, and analyzing the data to create the empathy map.

I created for Absolute 3rd Year

Think & feel

- Will I get a job

- college offers only coding or programming jobs

- Can I clear Aptitude round?

- Will I get a job under my passion niche

- Can I grow myself and independent?

See

- My friends are getting placed in MNC’s for entry level job

- I couldn’t see good recommending jobsite without fake job listings.

- I can see many people are getting a good pay in my field as a fresher

- Freelancing are in trend

- There are years of bond more than 2 years

Say & Do

- Attending workshops and events to secure a job

- Listening to random persons and following them

- Still trying to get job in his interest

- Asking for referral in big MNC for job

- Creating case study and getting mentored from industry person So that he could attend interviews.

Hear

- Faculties suggesting to get placed in on campus

- Friends are planning to go for higher studies for more specific study work

- Going for a any job for now is good due to recession

- To try alternate instead of his passionate work

### Pain and Gain

Pain

- If I couldn’t get a job I should go for walk in interviews it will be hassle

- I may get a job from my university , what if it is a BPO or any call center jobs?

- I have to grew with my family , I couldn’t secure a good job

- Walk in drive may have more rounds of interview

- Nobody is hiring for freshers

- Top tier colleges are getting better packages

Gain

- Using AI enabled jobsites can be useful

- Applying for multiple jobs through good jobsite

- Lot of features with a jobsite can filter out my resume without ATS

- I can apply for more jobs till I get the perfect one

- Quick apply to jobs without filling from scratch

- Detailed job description for all type of job listings

## Ideate

After making all research with above , Lets indulge all into a idea were we discuss about the solution. This phase is called ideation where this can bring up solution to the problem and as well as idea for making it optimized.

Pain point - Responsive Layout

- This website sucks in Tab and Mobile versions

- Users can’t take updates through mobile phones

Solution

- The visual appealing of a design should be usable for all devices , So the users can use through all devices

- Use popular JS frameworks such as React to make it seamless and quick response

Pain Point - Visual Design

- The old monster website was clean and natural , It was easy to use than the new rebranded Foundit.

- I can’t see some features that allows me to do such task here.

Solution

- Try to make some references from old website when ever rebranded into new , So that you can pull old as well as new users to daily traffic

- Foundit website should do color theory tinkering with purple color

- Aesthetics of design should be improved all over the website using design kits.

Pain Point - Business View

- Foundit header has list of features where it has a tag of “Premium Services” , So the users who are students clearly get that it is a paid thing and the will not explore about whatever you have there

- There are lot of services which will really helpful to students and freshers are not mentioned in landing page.

- Bad Consumer support from foundIt and no basic chat bot support

- Unverified job resources and inserting lot of premium services

Solution

- Remove Premium , So it will give chance to at least to explore the services foundIt offers to its users.

- Adding a basic dialog flow chatbot , So I can prepare for worst case for users and provide basic support

- Job postings are done with multiple verifications and It should be same website as foundit

## Prototype

In the ever-changing world of UX, effective prototyping pipelines have shown to be the cornerstone that minimizes wasted resources and reduces the need for iterative design processes. It protects against missed opportunities. The above prototype is based on Foundit’s design guidelines and I have made for both desktop and mobile versions. This is the basic step before going into fully functional design where it can trace out the needed thing such as layout of the website.

title: GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite
text:
Note:

Hi, I am Abusyeed from Pondicherry. I writing this study for an challenge called Dan Jr Hackathon organized by GUVI. In this project my role is UX designer and I took 2 days of time starting from 23/1/24 - 25/1/24 to complete this whole landing page, tools like Adobe Dimension, Figma, Fig Jam, Adobe Illustrator are used in this project, In addition I took 7 coffees till the end of the project.

I have skipped the research part because it is a assessment and the objective is to design the page according to given.

Happy Reading!

## 01 Introduction

People have little time to devote to their health in this fast-paced environment. In today's corporate culture, people order food from restaurants on a daily basis to save time, but they overlook an important aspect: their health. Yes, I'm referring to a user's calorie intake in their daily life. They are unaware of how many calories they are consuming or how it will impact them in the future. To address this issue, GreenBite was founded, and they offer a variety of organic pre-meal kits tailored to a user's specific nutrient needs, such as carbs, fiber, protein, etc.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled.png)

Introduction Image

### About GreenBite

GreenBite is a fictional organic meal kit delivery service that focuses on providing customers with fresh, high-quality ingredients for easy and nutritious home-cooked meals. The primary goal is to promote a healthier lifestyle by providing a carefully curated selection of organic produce and meal kits, making it easier to prepare delicious, nutritious meals.

### Statement

As I mentioned before, I am writing this for an event, so they have given me the challenge as

Your task is to design the landing page for "GreenBite," a fictional organic food delivery service. The service focuses on delivering fresh, organic produce and healthy meal kits to customers' doorsteps. Your design should reflect the brand's commitment to health, sustainability, and convenience. Design for both desktop and mobile version.

## 02 Goal

The goal is to create a landing page that caters to all types of users and makes them feel at ease when using Green Bite's services. There are objectives provided, such as

- User Engagement: Create a visually appealing and intuitive interface that engages users from the first glance.

- Brand Representation: Incorporate elements that represent health, organic produce, and sustainability.

- Clear Call-to-Action (CTA): Design a prominent CTA that encourages users to sign up or explore products.

So I'll be designing GreenBite with all of the aforementioned considerations in mind. I'll walk you through it from inspiration to how I ended up creating this page.

### Core Objective

- User Centric Design

- User Persona

- Visual Design

- Responsiveness

## 03 The Approach

Design Process

- Empathize

- Define

- Ideate

- Prototype

- Testing

### Empathize

Although the empathize phase should be based on exhaustive testing by end users, I am using online sources to analyze insights from qualitative and quantitative user research. This is basically a collection of assumptions and considerations. I used the basic design thinking process, but in this case study, as I mentioned, I didn't research, but we were able to identify problems that will affect people.

### Define

In this section, I defined a type of person; because this is a user-centric approach, it will undoubtedly help to create a better landing page. This can lead to more accessible and usable designs.

Type

- Health Conscious

- Corporate Trapped

### Health Conscious

As the person will be more health conscious for themselves and for their family and have chances to make that as routine if the services are in daily plan, so will see this in a brief manner. I took one my friend for this persona achieving better understanding.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 1.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 2.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 3.png)

### Corporate Trapped

These type of users have very hectic lifestyle and have very minimum time to have healthy food, Maybe GreenBite can help them to eat healthy organic food on daily basis according to their needs.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 4.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 5.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 6.png)

### Ideate

I thoroughly read the pains and frustrations of the persona and came up with many ideas to solve and for designing this landing page. In terms of color schemes, I had a clear mental image that words like organic and sustainable could be associated with green or shades of green. I also have the required header menu from the given, as they specified it should be minimal.

Logo

Initially, I created a logo because it is everything that constitutes an overall structure and is a wordmark. I simplified it by using basic font settings that express green and bite visually, similar to "GreenBite".

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Logo_Explanation.png)

Logo and Brand

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Food_Packet.png)

Takeout Box

Takeout box

I created a takeout box in Adobe Dimension and inserted the package design into the takeout box to make it more visually appealing. There are many templates on the internet, but I couldn't find the angle I was looking for, so I created it myself. The packet contains information such as the price, name of the product, and a nutrition table.

Initial Ideas

I created a hero section with an image that contains a lot of information at first glance, so that even new users can get an idea of the product or service they provide. I kept a headline to capture the attention of users, and it worked very well when tested, as you will see in the following topics. To highlight the primary CTA, I used the normal brand color. It is placed in the center of the landing page to encourage users to explore further and learn more about the different types or categories of products they offer.

Visual Appeal

As previously stated, I have included an image of a takeout meal as well as a customer review. This instills trust in users when they see the benefits of the product and decide to proceed without hesitation. Down the page, I added products that users can purchase if they are interested in the product. To capture their attention, I used phrases like "Top Seller" and "Value Pack" on specific products.

In addition, I mentioned the brand and their goal of eradicating fast food culture while empowering all users with nutritious food. So these elements are included to build trust with both new and existing users.

Components & Interaction

I also created components in the project for both primary and secondary CTAs, as well as for the website alone, to distinguish between pressed and hover states. There is another component used in the header menu with animated bars underline, this scroll down easily to the required area. In addition, the mobile version of the website includes a hamburger icon, which is used to group the header menu.

I've made the header interactive so that you can hover to see what you're hovering over, and it will scroll through the page based on the menu. nu for easier usability on small mobile phones.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 7.png)

Color & Typography

I used a dark shade of green and light green for better contrast. As usual I used greys for dividers and pressed animation with slight color change.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Colors.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Typography.png)

### Prototype

Hi-fidelity wireframe

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Tab_Wireframe.png)

Tab

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Mobile_Wireframe.png)

Mobile

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Lo-Fi_Wireframe.png)

Low fidelity wireframe

Figma Prototype

In this you will find the prototype of the website with all interactions. Flow 1, Flow 2, Flow 3 are the three different size such as desktop, tab, mobile.

https://www.figma.com/proto/5HZpzvf0FsVZWqBFiHOPjR/GUVI?page-id=0%3A1&type=design&node-id=1-2&viewport=210%2C190%2C0.15&t=RDQTZxiqqOu8oCHT-1&scaling=scale-down&starting-point-node-id=1%3A2&mode=design

Final Screens

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_17.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_14.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_15.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_16.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Footer.png)

Footer

## 04 Testing

I always use Maze or Useberry to test my designs with real users; this will undoubtedly reveal potential changes that can be made as an iteration in design to improve the usability of the design. Instead we can evaluate the heuristics of the design with usability laws.

As it is a fictional, I gave a try to attention insight plugin for heatmaps and it has given lot of insights.

- Headline - 34.5%

- Image - 25.6%

- Primary CTA - 4.8%

- Logo - 2.8%

- Secondary CTA - 1.4%

So this is AI-generated, but it has a good algorithm that clearly analyzes the actual website. I received an optimal score of 66, which is ideal for a landing page. In this case, the headline is longer than average and provides a clear description of the product. In addition the image has around 25% of coverage and it is highlighted in the review section which will build trust to the user

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/heatmap.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Rectangle.png)

## 05 Recollect

I really like the idea of delivering good, healthy food to people. This is a fictional idea, but it contains an excellent solution to a problem that will arise in the near future. It has the potential to be a great initiative if it is widely implemented. Coming to the project experience, I had no hesitation in bringing ideas because I trusted the process, so I quickly grasped and gave my all in terms of user interface and user experience.

Through this project, I was able to revisit Adobe Dimension, which I had stopped using a year ago but found to be helpful in this instance when making takeout box models. In these two days, I learned how to quickly sketch out ideas and visuals much more than the old process. Overall working on this project, I gained good experience.

Thank you for reading till the end. Have a great day!

title: Kynhood - UX & AI
text:
## 01 Introduction

Hi, I am Abusyeed. I hope the reader is doing well. I am writing this document for the UX design role at Kynhood.

In this UX document, I have enhanced the zone and area selection feature of a fictional social media application, where the users couldn’t select the area and multiple zones it covers. I have added my solution in the context of the Kynhood app and a generic social media app, but moreover, it adds context to the app itself. In this document, I have given multiple solutions to problems, even with development-based and UI functionalities.

Role - Product DesignerMembers - SoloDuration - 48 HoursTools - Figma, Notion, Jupyter NotebookCoffee - 5 Cups

Thanks for giving me a opportunity to showcase my skills

https://www.figma.com/design/j2YLGjFpBMny0kUMs7jAyf/KYN?node-id=0-1&t=Xdz64VXLAnzX5TbL-1

## 02 Problem Statement

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled.png)

Source - Public Information Portal, Chennai

The main takeaway I got while reading was that users are unable to see which specific areas each zone covers, leading to confusion and usability issues. I had more ideas than designs to implement. I also had a look at the Kynhood app, and I got further ideas to implement. Moreover, the problem , It is indeed to design with accessibility as well. I got to see guidelines and much more. The problem statement also had a few things to cover more specifically, such as

Zone and Area Clarity - Design a solution that clearly displays all available zones and the respective areas they cover, ensuring users can easily understand their choices.

User-friendly Interaction - The method for selecting a zone should be straightforward, minimizing steps and complexity for the user.

Scalability - The design should accommodate future expansion, allowing for the addition of more zones and areas as the service grows into new cities and states.

Intuitive Design - The screen should intuitively convey its purpose and how to use it, ensuring users understand they are selecting a zone to receive content relevant to that area.

## 03 Initial Finding

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Image_16.png)

To solve this, first I need to know about the geography and how the nation divides zones, divisions, and wards. So I got to see websites that show divisions and zones of the city, and eventually, after so much research, I got an idea to work on. The geography of how the government has been divided has a list of wards for each zone. Certainly, some years ago, the wards would have multiple zones under the parliamentary or even assembly constituencies, but the newly proposed zones consist of distinct zones for each other.

For every 2000 to 3000 households, it is named as a ward, and most people would really remember their respective area name primitively, and the problem arrives now. As they are only aware of the wards or areas in which they live, they find it difficult to identify the zones that their area covers.

## 04 Ideation

I quickly took my note and drafted how zones get connected in the area, and it gave me a tree structure with multiple areas connected. As we know, the people know the primitive area where they live, so getting their area as input and mapping the zone would really help them identify the zone they come across.

It was more likely to work on the development side instead of the design side. As a product designer, I also want to look at how the back systems align with the seamless user experience.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 1.png)

Source - Wikipedia

I have taken Chennai’s wards and zones from Wikipedia, If see the above image, You can see the old and new divisions, which certainly will help us to navigate users with neighborhood content.

For instance,

If you closely look at these ward numbers, they aren’t continuous or sequential. If it has a range of 1–22, the missing ward comes under the second zone. If you can see the 1st zone, the 15–17 are missing, and the 2nd zone has those wards. In this way, if we could feed our database by mapping these wards to their respective zones, then eventually the users could get their own choice to select their zone. So if the users type their area, the zone can be easily identified. If a user who resides near a zone border won’t get content information, it's better to add nearest neighbor zones to the ward and give users the users the choice  to select zones.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 2.png)

Source - Chennai Greater Corporation

I have referred to the wards and zones from Chennai Corporation and found that the area names are distinct with the ward number, and if it exists in over 3000 households, the ward name changes with a distinct ward number. Although we consider the zone alone, it is distinct all the time

Ward NoZone NoAreaZoneNearest Zone

11KATHIVAKKAMTHIRUVOTRIYURTHIRUVOTRIYUR

152EDYANCHAVADIMANALITHIRUVOTRIYUR, MANALI

322KAVANKARAIMADHAVARAMMANALI, MADHAVARAM

## Design

I made the designs through my design system, which I made solely in my previous internship. I have designed for all type of users also to reduce cognitive load I have used normal input fields to take users input. Basically as we discussed, We will take the primitive input, which is area and will move on to identify the zone through area.

### User Flow

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 3.png)

User flow

This is the only way to complete the goal, because the location selection has 2 components alone and those sub elements are more effective to reach users goal soon.

The initial page would be the location page, the user then allowed to type the area name which is very primitive thing. After typing, the user gets options through a drop down menu to select their indeed area under their common zone

### Wireframe

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Image_17.png)

As soon as the ideas I got through, I roughly started drawing in my note with mostly all test cases, I found some ideas to keep some to remove. As I mentioned, before the design system, It took me like 5 mins to drag and drop components.

https://www.figma.com/design/3XEIfBgCzEuCkfnDGq4Li1/Design-System?node-id=132-8171&t=UcpCXACaA1QbXkps-1 - 786920

### High Fidelity

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 4.png)

1: It is an input text. It also has a label and a state text to keep the user informed. It also has a primary icon, which would tell the user to type and search the area.

2: Down the area input, we have zone input. There are mental modalities people will surely follow to fill the input fields from beginning to end linearly.

3: As soon as they type, a drop-down menu will offer them the option the option to select their area, despite the typo error. It also has a subheading above the area to mention the common zone name because there can be multiple areas with the same name.

4: The state text will help the user stay informed about the next step, which is to select or choose among the choices.

It would be better for users if we could add a tooltip stating the process in a minimal way

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 5.png)

6: As soon as the user types the area, they can find the neighborhood zone and also have a choice of selection. This selection gives them the freedom to select themselves. Mostly every user will get information from a information from a single zone nearby or where they live, but there are people who reside on the border of the zones who also wish to know information from both sides.

7: In the selection, the user needs to tick the checkbox, The reason I chose Checkbox is due to its mental model. Mostly, this is very common among every age group for selecting a group of products or anything. A checkbox is something used to change later in the process, but not the toggle switch.

8: You will get an active CTA button for action once you fill out the area and zone.

9: The user will have the option to see what they have selected in both the input field and the tag group. This will widely help users delete if they don’t need to.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 6.png)

10: It is a use case when only one zone is shown in the drop-down menu.

## 06 Suggestions

### AI

This could be feasible for structured cities with zones, and even if it changes dynamically and the data changes frequently, then these mappings should also be changed. To reduce the tedious task, we can use ML algorithms to overcome these issues.

I have come up with KNN - which is mostly used to find the nearest point between two cluster of data. We can implement a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifier to find the nearest zones based on a given ward number. It works by calculating the distance between the ward co-ords and other wards in the dataset. The algorithm then identifies the K nearest data points and assigns the majority class among them to the input data point. The KNN classifier determines the nearest zones to a given ward number by considering its neighboring wards and their corresponding zones.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/k-nearest-neighbor-algorithm-for-machine-learning11.png)

Source - Javatpoint

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 7.png)

Source - Google

Code

import numpy as np

from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier

zones = {

"THIRUVOTRIYUR": range(1, 14),

"MANALI": range(15, 21),

"MADHAVARAM": range(22, 33),

"TONDIARPET": range(34, 48),

"ROYAPURAM": range(49, 63),

"THIRUVIKA NAGAR": range(64, 78),

"AMBATTUR": range(79, 93),

"ANNA NAGAR": range(94, 108),

"TEYNAMPET": range(109, 126),

"KODAMBAKKAM": range(127, 142),

"VALASARAVAKKAM": range(143, 155),

"ALANDUR": range(156, 167),

"ADYAR": range(170, 182),

"PERUNGUDI": list(range(168, 169)) + list(range(183, 191)),

"SOZHANGANALLUR": range(192, 200)

}

ward_numbers = []

zone_names = []

for zone, wards in zones.items():

for ward in wards:

ward_numbers.append(ward)

zone_names.append(zone)

X = np.array(ward_numbers).reshape(-1, 1)

y = np.array(zone_names)

knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=3)

knn.fit(X, y)

def classify_ward(ward_number):

ward_number = np.array(ward_number).reshape(-1, 1)

prediction = knn.predict(ward_number)

neighbors = knn.kneighbors(ward_number, return_distance=False)

nearest_zones = list(set([y[i] for i in neighbors[0]]))

return prediction[0], nearest_zones

ward_number = int(input("Enter ward number: "))

zone, nearest_zones = classify_ward(ward_number)

print(f"Ward Number: {ward_number}")

print(f"Zone: {zone}")

print(f"Nearest Zones: {nearest_zones}")

The code sets up a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifier to predict zones based on ward numbers. It first organizes the data into arrays for training, then trains the KNN model with 3 neighbors to learn the relationship between ward numbers and zones. When a user inputs a ward number, the model predicts its zone and identifies the nearest zones using the KNN algorithm, providing a quick and accurate way to determine zones and their proximity based on ward numbers.

Output

Enter ward number: 14 - User would Type "RajaKadai"

Ward Number: 14

Zone: THIRUVOTRIYUR

Nearest Zones: ['MANALI', 'THIRUVOTRIYUR'] - User gets choice to select

As you can see from this output, if the user types the area they are aware of, at the moment the nearest and perfect place they lie can be easily determined. It has a dependency on the developers to maintain it and generate it according to various cities; moreover, according to the above method, it can easily identify zones.

### Maps

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 8.png)

Adding maps to the interface will definitely help users who recently moved to a city. The map can actually refer to pins while the area is typed and can show an outline to wards, so they can easily select their area and zone.

## 07 Conclusion

This assignment gave me the opportunity to think about and revise the geographic specifications, of which I wasn’t aware enough. So far, I have learned about wards, divisions, and zones and how they are partitioned. To be honest, this assignment was unique in terms of my previous intern assignment.

Coming to the future of scaling, As I said, it may change from time to time, and it is not the way we could change the datasets each time. Using ML algorithms and advancing would be more efficient and time-saving.
